stories.title: Trump Aims to Calm Lebanon Tensions, Keep Talks Alive
stories.summary: President Donald Trump and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu have offered differing accounts of a call about the fighting in Lebanon, as the US struggles to get efforts toward an Iran peace deal back on track. Bloomberg's Laura Davison has the latest. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: South Korea Overtakes India as World’s Sixth-Largest Stock Market
stories.summary: South Korea’s equity market has overtaken India’s as the world’s sixth largest, driven by a relentless surge in chip heavyweights powering the global artificial intelligence buildout.

stories.title: Bloom CEO Has No Plans to Sell Shares After AI Investor Run-up
stories.summary: Bloom Energy Corp., a supplier to Oracle Corp. that’s seen its stock price double in the past two months, doesn’t see a need to sell shares to meet surging demand from data centers, its chief executive officer says.

stories.title: Nvidia's Huang on South Korean Partnerships
stories.summary: Jensen Huang speaks to reporters in Taipei about the high potential of South Korean tech companies. He speaks as Nvidia enters the PC market with a new chip aimed at loosening the stranglehold of Intel technology in that arena. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Oil Holds Biggest Gain in a Month on Standoff in US-Iran Talks
stories.summary: Oil steadied after its biggest gain in about a month, as uncertainty about the state of US-Iran peace talks raised the risk that energy flows from the Persian Gulf could be curtailed for longer.
